hero.heading: @love-rox/tcy-astro
hero.tagline: Astro 4+ only. Auto-registers the rehype plugin on Astro's Markdown / MDX pipeline, and ships a `<Tcy>` component for `.astro` files.

install.heading: Install
install.description: Peer dependency: `astro >= 4`. `@love-rox/tcy-core` and `@love-rox/tcy-rehype` are pulled in automatically.

items.name: tcy() integration
items.description: Registers `@love-rox/tcy-rehype` on Astro's Markdown / MDX pipeline so half-width alphanumerics in your `.md` and `.mdx` content are wrapped automatically at build time.

items.name: <Tcy> component
items.description: An Astro component to wrap a slot inside `.astro` files explicitly — useful for body content authored directly in `.astro`, outside the Markdown pipeline.

usage.heading: Usage
usage.description: Register the integration in `astro.config.mjs`. That alone makes Markdown / MDX content tate-chu-yoko-aware.
usage.withOptionsDescription: Forward any `rehype-tcy` options through the integration call.
usage.componentDescription: For body content authored directly in `.astro` (no Markdown in the picture), use the `<Tcy>` component.

options.heading: Options
options.description: The shared options behave exactly as they do in the React / Vue / rehype adapters. There's just one integration-only knob.

behavior.heading: Behavior notes
items: Astro is SSG-first, so both pieces run at build time — there's no client-side cost.
Text inside `<code>` / `<pre>` / `<script>` / `<style>` is skipped by default (configurable via `skipTags`).
**Idempotent**. Rebuild the same Markdown as many times as you want; the output HTML doesn't drift.
The `<Tcy>` component delegates to the same `tcy-rehype` underneath, so Markdown content and `.astro`-authored content behave identically.

related.heading: Other adapters
related.description: Not using Astro? Drop the [rehype plugin](./rehype) directly into your `unified` pipeline. Need runtime wrapping in the app? Use the [React](./react) or [Vue](./vue) component.
